prose:
Method-provenance summary
Property: HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans orbital energy difference), eV
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis set (tier “standard”), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF v?.
Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry supplied by user (file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz); no geometry optimization was performed – this is a single-point orbital calculation denoted DFT//exp-geom.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet, RHF/RKS reference).
Software: chemkit frontier-orbitals skill (engine subcommand `frontier`, PySCF backend); SCF converged in 8 cycles.
Convergence & warnings: SCF converged; no warnings reported by engine.
Live log: the run streamed to a time-stamped file named frontier_<timestamp>.out in the working directory (available for inspection); JSON results stored in benzene_frontier.json.

Result
HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6310857 eV (HOMO −7.0468329 eV, LUMO −0.41574728 eV)

This value was computed with PySCF at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level on the supplied benzene geometry. The calculation’s integrity check reports status = ok, trustworthy = true.

Provenance: computed
